target_game.name: Color Road!
target_game.core_loop: Steer a rolling ball along a track, matching its color with obstacles to pass through while avoiding mismatched colors and collecting coins.
target_game.audience_proxy: Casual gamers of all ages looking for quick, reflex-based gameplay.
target_game.visual_style: Minimalist 3D with abstract geometric shapes and a soft pastel background.

palette.description: High contrast between the dark track and soft pastel background, with bright, saturated colors for interactive elements.

top_archetypes.label: Fail · in-game

game_fit_scores.rationale: This is a licensed-IP anime hero shot for a gacha RPG — it has nothing in common with Color Road's minimalist abstract geometry, pastel palette, or reflex color-matching loop. Beyond the obvious IP/legal blocker, the saturated rainbow + character-charging-energy hook signals a mid-core anime RPG audience, which is a poor overlap with hypercasual reflex players and would attract low-LTV, low-intent installs. Do not adapt; skip this archetype entirely.

Hard pass. The archetype is anime IP fan-art (Goku Dragon Fist) with deep gold/indigo/purple tones and a power-fantasy beat — none of which maps to Color Road's minimalist pastel runner aesthetic, color-matching loop, or all-ages casual audience. Beyond the stylistic mismatch, using DBZ likeness creates clear IP/legal risk and would mislead installers, tanking retention. Skip unless rebuilt from scratch as an abstract "satisfying ball smash" beat using the game's own palette.

The "relaxation/satisfaction" puzzle-sort hook clashes with Color Road!'s reflex-driven, energetic runner loop — players come for fast color-matching dodge action, not slow top-down sorting, so a misleading bus-puzzle hook risks high install-but-churn cost and potential ad-network policy flags. Palette is also off (warm orange/blue/purple vs. Color Road's pastel + dark track signature), and there's no rolling-ball or color-gate imagery to anchor recognition. Audience overlap on broad hypercasual/casual demo is the only redeeming axis; recommend skipping or, if testing, restage the satisfaction angle around oddly-satisfying ball+color-gate ASMR footage instead of bus-sort.

This documentary-style "secret bunker reveal" hook is fundamentally incompatible with Color Road!'s abstract minimalist 3D rolling-ball gameplay — there's no narrative, landmark, or cross-section element in the game to anchor the curiosity payoff. The earthy bunker palette (#2c3e50/#d35400/#2d5a27) also clashes with the game's bright pastel-and-saturated-accent look. Skip this archetype; curiosity hooks for Color Road! should be built around "will the ball make it?" near-fail moments or color-switch puzzles, not landmark/lore reveals.

The "fail bait" hook concept (cursor-driven mistake + provocative "can you win?" text) is a proven hypercasual UA pattern that overlaps with Color Road!'s broad casual audience, but the specific execution is a poor fit: buses, crashes, and a green/red/white palette have no relationship to the game's minimalist pastel rolling-ball-and-color-match DNA. Mechanic match is weak because steering a bus into traffic doesn't translate the color-matching/dodging core loop — viewers who click expecting traffic puzzles will mis-install. Recommendation: borrow the fail-bait STRUCTURE (cursor steers ball into wrong-colored obstacle, "why is this so hard?" overlay) but do not reuse this asset; rebuild it natively in the game's pastel/dark-track aesthetic.

brief.title: "Actually Relaxing?" — Color Road! ASMR Restage
brief.hook_3s: "Ever played a game that ACTUALLY relaxes you?" — finger glides a pink ball through a perfect pink color-gate, crisp click on impact.
scene_flow: 0–3s: Close-up POV of a thumb steering a pastel-pink ball down the dark track; ball threads a matching pink gate with a satisfying click. VO question hooks the viewer.
3–7s: Pull back — chain of 5 color gates (pink → red → pink) cleared in rhythm, coins vacuumed up, combo counter climbs. Each match pops a soft chime.
7–11s: Tension beat — a mismatched red wall approaches while ball is pink; thumb swipes sideways at the last frame to grab a red paint-splash power-up, ball flips red, smashes through. Score juice.
11–14s: Speed ramp — long satisfying run of perfect matches, screen blooms pastel, coin counter ticks up fast.
14–15s: End card — ball rolls into giant glossy logo; CTA stamps in over pastel city skyline.
brief.visual_direction: Authentic Color Road! in-game framing: deep navy (#152238) ribbon track floating over a soft pastel-pink (#FADADD) blurred city skyline, saturated #FF4C4C accents on hazard gates and the score HUD. Minimalist 3D abstract geometry — no characters, just the rolling ball, color gates, coin pickups, and the standard top-right pause button + top-left score counter. Camera is the signature low-chase angle behind the ball, with one tight thumb-on-screen overlay shot to sell the "oddly satisfying" tactile angle. Soft bloom, shallow DOF on background buildings, crisp specular highlight on the ball.
text_overlays: Ever played a game that ACTUALLY relaxes you?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Color Road! rotation is all unlabeled Applovin playables/videos with no clear emotional angle — the satisfaction/ASMR-question hook is trending market-wide but absent here. We restage the bus-sort archetype's "relaxing question" VO onto Color Road!'s native rolling-ball + color-gate ASMR footage, which keeps install intent honest (no bait-and-switch churn) while stealing the scroll-stopping hook. UA: test against the existing playables as a top-funnel video; if CTR lifts, port the same VO into a playable variant.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic Color Road! in-game screenshot: glossy pastel-pink rolling ball mid-track on a dark navy #152238 ribbon track, threading a perfectly matched pink color-gate with a tiny burst of #FF4C4C sparks, soft pastel #FADADD blurred city skyline background, minimalist 3D abstract geometric shapes, no characters, top-left score counter '0124' and top-right pause button visible, low chase camera, shallow depth of field, soft bloom, on-screen text 'Ever played a game that ACTUALLY relaxes you?' in clean white sans-serif at upper third. [Audio: VO 'Ever played a game that actually relaxes you?' / chill lo-fi-meets-energetic 95 BPM pastel beat / soft click on gate pass at 1.5s, coin chime at 2.5s]

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, Color Road! gameplay still: long dark #152238 track snaking over pastel #FADADD city, pink ball mid-roll about to hit a #FF4C4C red wall while a red paint-splash pickup glows just left, combo counter 'x8' pulsing top-center, coin trail behind ball, minimalist 3D abstract geometric style, no characters, signature pause button top-right, on-screen text '…and don't blink 👀' bottom third in white. [Audio: VO whisper 'don't blink' / rising 100 BPM competitive synth swell / whoosh on swipe at 2s, deep thud-then-shatter on red wall break at 3.5s]

Storyboard end-card frame, aspect 9:16, Color Road! style: pastel-pink ball resting beside oversized glossy 'Color Road!' wordmark on dark navy #152238 track, pastel #FADADD sky, scattered #FF4C4C confetti, minimalist 3D abstract geometry, no characters, big white 'Play Free' CTA button center-bottom, score '999+' and coin icon top HUD, on-screen text 'Your brain will thank you' upper third. [Audio: VO 'Your brain will thank you — play free' / triumphant 110 BPM pastel-pop sting / cash-register coin cascade at 1s, soft button-tap pop on CTA at 3s]

brief.title: "Why Is This Level So Hard?" — Pastel Fail Bait
brief.hook_3s: A clumsy finger drags a pink ball straight into a red wall — SMASH — pastel shards explode across the dark track.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Cold open on the dark track. A giant cartoon hand-cursor grabs the rolling pastel-pink ball. Overlay slams in: 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?'
3-7s: Cursor steers the pink ball directly into a RED obstacle wall. Buzzer. Ball shatters into pastel shards. Overlay: '99% FAIL HERE 😬'
7-11s: Replay attempt — cursor wobbles, ball clips a mismatched accent-red gate, misses three coin stacks. Overlay: 'STILL CAN'T DO IT?'
11-14s: Speed ramp: a clean run shows the ball flipping through matching pink/red/navy gates, snagging coins, score counter spiking. Overlay: 'CAN YOU BEAT IT?'
14-15s: Freeze on score screen with pastel city skyline behind. CTA pulses.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to Color Road!'s native look: deep navy (#152238) ribbon track snaking through a soft pastel-pink (#FADADD) sky with abstract geometric shapes (floating cubes, pylons, arches) in the background. The ball and gate colors pop in saturated accent red (#FF4C4C) and pastel pink. No characters. Keep the real HUD chrome — score counter top-left, pause button top-right, coin tally — exactly as it appears in-game. Cursor is an oversized white glove-style pointer, intentionally clumsy. Fail moments use a quick red flash + pastel shard burst; success moments use chromatic gate-pass shimmer. Camera stays in the signature low-chase angle behind the ball.
text_overlays: WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Color Road! rotation is all uncaptioned playables and silent gameplay videos — zero fail-bait or text-driven provocation hooks, despite fail-bait being a proven hypercasual UA pattern. This brief imports the trending "cursor crashes, can you win?" structure but rebuilds it natively in the pastel/dark-track DNA so click-through audiences land on a matching mechanic (no bus-crash mis-install). Ship as a 15s video first; if CTR lifts, port the same beat structure into a playable variant to refresh the AppLovin slot dated 2024-10-09.
scenario_prompts: 9:16 vertical hero frame, authentic Color Road! in-game moment: a glossy pastel-pink rolling ball speeding along a winding dark navy (#152238) ribbon track, a giant cartoon white hand-cursor clumsily dragging the ball off-line toward a saturated accent-red (#FF4C4C) wall obstacle directly ahead, soft pastel-pink (#FADADD) sky background dotted with abstract floating geometric cubes and arches, native HUD visible: score counter top-left, pause button top-right, small coin tally, minimalist 3D with abstract geometric shapes and a soft pastel background, bold white sans-serif overlay text centered top: 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?', no characters, low chase camera angle behind the ball. [Audio: VO (frustrated female, 22yo): 'Wait — why is this so hard?!' / energetic competitive 128 BPM punchy electro-pop with hand-clap percussion / cursor-grab whoosh at 0.5s, ominous low hum building under text slam]

9:16 vertical storyboard frame, authentic Color Road! fail beat: the pastel-pink ball mid-shatter into bright pastel shards and accent-red (#FF4C4C) sparks after smashing a mismatched red gate on the dark navy (#152238) track, motion blur lines, scattered uncollected coins bouncing off-track, soft pastel-pink (#FADADD) sky with abstract geometric shapes, native HUD score counter frozen and pause button top-right, minimalist 3D abstract geometric style, big bold white overlay text bottom-center: '99% FAIL HERE 😬', no characters, dramatic low angle. [Audio: VO: 'Nope.' / same 128 BPM competitive track drops out for impact / loud cartoon fail-buzzer + glass-shard tinkle SFX at 0.3s, beat re-enters at 1.5s]

9:16 vertical storyboard frame, authentic Color Road! success beat: the pastel-pink ball flips through a perfectly matched pink color gate while a saturated accent-red (#FF4C4C) ball-state passes a red gate next, golden coins streaming into the score counter, dark navy (#152238) ribbon track curving through a soft pastel-pink (#FADADD) sky filled with abstract geometric pylons and cubes, native HUD with rising score top-left and pause button top-right, minimalist 3D with abstract geometric shapes and a soft pastel background, bold white overlay text top: 'CAN YOU BEAT IT?', no characters, dynamic chase camera. [Audio: VO (confident): 'Think you can?' / 128 BPM energetic competitive synth-pop swelling to peak / rapid coin-chime combo SFX, satisfying gate-pass shimmer at 0.4s and 1.8s, riser into CTA]

brief.title: What's Hiding Under the Track?
brief.hook_3s: The pastel city splits open — what's beneath the Color Road track?
scene_flow: 0-3s: Rolling ball cruises a dark track over pastel city skyline; camera suddenly tilts and the world cross-sections open like a diorama, revealing the track is actually floating on stacked layers below.

3-7s: Camera pans DOWN through the cross-section — layer 1 shows hidden coin vaults, layer 2 shows a giant color-swap machine pumping pink/red paint into the track, layer 3 shows a maze of failed mismatched balls piled up.
7-11s: Snap back to gameplay POV — ball charges a wall of red blocks, swaps to red just in time (near-fail), shatters through them in a satisfying burst of pastel particles.
11-14s: Cross-section flashes back ONE more time to show the secret 'next level' chamber lighting up as score multiplier ticks: x2 → x5 → x10.
14-15s: CTA card with rolling ball icon and tap prompt.
brief.visual_direction: Lean fully into Color Road!'s minimalist 3D language — dark navy (#152238) ribbon track suspended over a soft pastel pink (#FADADD) city silhouette, with saturated accent red (#FF4C4C) for the rolling ball and matching gates. Adapt the "cross-section reveal" archetype by literally slicing the diorama open: clean orthogonal cuts, abstract geometric chambers stacked vertically, no realism or grit. Keep the in-game HUD intact (score counter top-left, pause button top-right, coin tally) so every frame reads as authentic Color Road! UI. Camera moves are snappy, almost toy-like; particle bursts on color matches stay bright and saturated against the muted pastel backdrop.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Color Road! rotation is all gameplay-loop video and playables with no narrative framing — this brief injects the trending curiosity/cross-section reveal archetype that's winning across the market but is absent from their lineup. The "what's under the track" twist gives the UA team a thumb-stopping hook that still anchors to authentic color-match gameplay, so it differentiates without breaking IP recognizability. Run as a 15s video alongside existing playables to test top-funnel CTR lift on curiosity-driven scrollers.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, 9:16 vertical aspect, minimalist 3D with abstract geometric shapes and a soft pastel background. A glossy saturated red (#FF4C4C) ball rolls along a dark navy (#152238) ribbon track that floats above a soft pastel pink (#FADADD) abstract city skyline. The world is sliced open in a clean orthogonal cross-section revealing three stacked underground chambers below the track: a coin vault glowing gold, a pink paint-pumping color engine, and a maze of mismatched ball wreckage. In-game HUD visible: score counter top-left, pause button top-right, coin tally. Bold white sans-serif overlay text reads 'What's UNDER the track?'. Crisp toy-diorama lighting, saturated accents, no characters, no realism. [Audio: VO (curious whisper) 'Wait... what's under the track?' / energetic competitive synth-pop at 128 BPM with rising arpeggio / ground-crack rumble at 1s, reveal whoosh at 2s, coin chime at 4s]

Coins streaming up into the ball above. Color Road HUD preserved: score counter, pause button, coin tally. Bold overlay text 'Combo x10!'. No characters, toy-diorama lighting.
